communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: TUHSD Race/Ethnicity Data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide any and all of the the following  that pertain to  the 2021-22, 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25, and/or 2025-26 academic years—

                    1.	Any and all records reflecting data bearing on students’ social, emotional, or psychological well-being, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	Youth Truth Survey results;
                                    b.	California Healthy Kids Survey results (CHKS); and
                                     c.	 Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific surveys or measures.

                    2.	Any and all records reflecting annual and/or semi-annual academic data, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	DFI Semester Grades;
                                    b.	Local Control and Accountability Plans (LCAP);
                                     c.	School Accountability Report Cards (SARC); and
                                    d.	Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific data that is or can be disaggregated by race.

                    3.	Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of the District pertaining to any of the aforementioned data.

Where school-specific data is disaggregated by grade, please provide combined data for all grades as well where available; our goal with this request is to ensure that racial/ethnic groups whose numbers are too small to be reportable at the grade level will reach reportable numbers when all grade levels are included in the data for a given school year.

Please provide any raw data in excel or csv format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tammy Edmonson

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Valencia - March 22
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am stating the purpose of this request so that you may assist me as required by California Government Code (“Gov”) § 7922.600(a)(1). The purpose of this California Public Records Act request is to determine the existence or non-existence of a specific record.

Please note that I am not requesting the record itself, only confirmation of its existence or non-existence. Thus, Civil Code § 1798.90.55, law enforcement exemptions, the public interest exemption or any other laws or exemptions do not apply to, and are not relevant to, this request.

Please confirm the existence or non-existence of the following Flock Safety ALPR footage record:

License Plate: WCD6448 Texas
Date: March 22, 2026
Time Frame: 11:15 AM - 11:25 AM
Camera Location: On East side of Valencia St, just North of Cesar Chavez St
Camera Lat/long: 37.74830951712188, -122.42020523544745

The following provisions of the CPRA are expected to apply to *all* elements of this request:

A:  Per Article 1, Section 3(b)(2) of the California Constitution, broadly construe all provisions that further the public’s right to access, and apply any limitations on access as narrowly as possible.

B:  Per §7922.535(a), catalog the responsive records or information, and describe their location. The identification of all records is applicable even if the record is withheld, pursuant to a lawful exemption to the CPRA, or redacted in whole or in part.

C:  Pursuant to this PRA request, and to your obligations under GOV § 7922.600(a), which obligates public agencies to assist the public to make focused and effective requests that reasonably describe identifiable records, with respect to all exempted or withheld documents I request assistance in the following ways:

a. Please assist me in identifying records and information responsive to the request or purpose of the request;

b. Please describe the information technology and physical location in which the records exist;

c. Please provide me with suggestions for overcoming any practical basis for denying access to the records or information sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

David Moore

title: FERPA Request for Education Records (Spring 2014 & Fall 2013)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: FERPA Request for Education Records (Spring 2014 & Fall 2013)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I, Jung, Young Jin (Student ID: 1442942), request access to all education records under the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) related to the following:

(1) Final Examination – Spring 2014
Records reflecting whether I took the final examination in Spring 2014, including but not limited to attendance records, exam logs, grading records, and any related documentation.

(2) Course Grading Criteria – Japanese 3 (Fall 2013)
Records reflecting the grading criteria for Japanese 3 in Fall 2013 under Professor Kanzo Takemori, including whether pop quizzes were included in the final grade calculation.

This request includes, but is not limited to, all relevant records such as syllabi, grading rubrics, course policies, records showing how my final grade was calculated, and any internal communications or documents used to determine grade calculation.

I request that these records be provided in electronic format where available.

This is a request for records, not a request for explanation or analysis.
If no responsive records exist, please provide a written statement confirming that no such records were created or maintained in the ordinary course of business.
To the extent any requested materials are maintained and directly relate to me, they constitute education records under FERPA.
This request is not limited to publicly distributed materials such as syllabi, but includes records reflecting how grading was actually implemented and applied in my case.
If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please produce all reasonably segregable portions and identify the specific legal basis for each withholding.
Failure to provide responsive records or a legally sufficient explanation for withholding may implicate my rights under FERPA and other applicable laws.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

title: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: EscambiaVotes GIS Mapping Data & IT Administration (2022-Present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian / Hon. Robert Bender, Escambia County Supervisor of Elections:

Under the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the public-facing election results portal hosted on or linked via EscambiaVotes.gov.

Publicly available data on the EscambiaVotes portal indicates a systemic, multi-year degradation of the GIS precinct mapping module. Specifically, the public map functioned correctly during the 2022 General Election (last updated 12/8/2022). However, it suffered a complete failure during the 2024 Primary and General Elections (displaying no map data), and remains only partially functional as of the 2025 Special Election (displaying broken or unlinked shapefiles).

Because the underlying tabular voting data is actively present on the site, this request seeks to audit the administrative failure of the GIS mapping API and the visual transparency tool itself. I am requesting the following records:

1. IT and Administrative Correspondence (Dec 2022 - Present):
Any internal IT helpdesk tickets, error logs, or administrative memorandums generated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding GIS shapefile errors, precinct redistricting desynchronization, or the failure of the mapping API to visually populate precinct data on the public portal.

2. Third-Party Vendor Communications:
Any communications (including emails, letters, or service requests) between the Escambia County Supervisor of Elections office and the third-party election software vendor/portal host (e.g., VR Systems) dated between December 8, 2022, and the present date regarding the broken map data, API error resolution, or portal maintenance for the 2024 and 2025 elections.

Statutory Compliance:
If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please advise me of any costs associated with fulfilling this request prior to processing.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Records Request
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
 The City of Minneapolis accepts requests for data under Minnesota State Statute Chapter 13 Minnesota Government Data Practices Act in the following ways:

*        Online using OpenCity, the City's online data request portal at https://www.minneapolismn.gov/government/government-data/request-public-data/. This site includes details about how to request public data along with other resources and information you may find helpful. Requests submitted this way will be assigned a case number that will allow you to track its progress going forward.

*        By phone, where a 311 agent can assist you by submitting a request on your behalf. 311 is the City's single access point for all non-emergency information and service requests. Agents can be reached Monday - Friday, from 7 a.m. - 7 p.m. by dialing 311, or 612-673-3000.

*        In person, at a kiosk in the City's Service Center, located at the skyway level of the City's Public Service Building, at 505 4th Avenue S. Minneapolis, MN 55415.
Thank you.

Courtney Martin l Enterprise Information Management Analyst  l  City of Minneapolis - City Clerk's Office  l courtney.martin@minneapolismn.gov<mailto:courtney.martin@minneapolismn.gov>

Learn more about Data Practices at the City of Minneapolis [here]<http://www.minneapolismn.gov/datapractices/>
You can submit requests, receive data, and check the status of your request using our Open City Portal [here]<https://minneapolis.service-now.com/opencityportal>
Contact our team at 612-673-5256 or by emailing us at ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov<mailto:ResponsibleAuthority@minneapolismn.gov>

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Meade County, Kentucky Jailer Training Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

For all Training Records for the current Meade County Jailer John J Scarborough, between 2014 to the date of processing this request.

The records responsive to this request are likely available in a database, or in some query-able format. If your department or agency does not have direct access to the records, please raise to the respective department that can run queries directly (eg, through SQL).

Should your agency not have all of the requested records within a single record or document, please provide me the relevant documents that do, even if that requires more than one class of document. Should some of the records be unavailable, please let me know ahead of time so that we can narrow the request accordingly.

As the records responsive to this request are imperative for understanding how search warrants are executed, I ask that the public interest of these records be evaluated to the extent that the public's interest outweighs this request's burden. To add, this request is being sent to the largest city of every state and will be collated together to create a comprehensive map of search warrants throughout the US. As such, the records are not only important for understanding search warrants at a local level, but also at a national and state-wide level.

Please ensure that provided documents comply with Section 508, as these records will be shared with members of the public who are covered under the ADA. This guide should be helpful: https://www.hhs.gov/web/section-508/making-files-accessible/checklist/pdf/index.html

If this request was sent to the wrong department or agency, please let me know so that I can send to the correct place.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Boquist @ Freeman Press

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel L Boquist

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: TUHSD Race/Ethnicity Data
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide any and all of the the following  that pertain to  the 2021-22, 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25, and/or 2025-26 academic years—

                    1.	Any and all records reflecting data bearing on students’ social, emotional, or psychological well-being, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	Youth Truth Survey results;
                                    b.	California Healthy Kids Survey results (CHKS); and
                                     c.	 Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific surveys or measures.

                    2.	Any and all records reflecting annual and/or semi-annual academic data, disaggregated by race/ethnicity where available, for the District as a whole and for each of the District’s high schools including—
                                    a.	DFI Semester Grades;
                                    b.	Local Control and Accountability Plans (LCAP);
                                     c.	School Accountability Report Cards (SARC); and
                                    d.	Any other Districtwide or school-site-specific data that is or can be disaggregated by race.

                    3.	Any and all reports, records, and analyses created by, for, or at the direction of the District pertaining to any of the aforementioned data.

Where school-specific data is disaggregated by grade, please provide combined data for all grades as well where available; our goal with this request is to ensure that racial/ethnic groups whose numbers are too small to be reportable at the grade level will reach reportable numbers when all grade levels are included in the data for a given school year.

Please provide any raw data in excel or csv format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

I am stating the purpose of this request so that you may assist me as required by California Government Code (“Gov”) § 7922.600(a)(1). The purpose of this California Public Records Act request is to determine the existence or non-existence of a specific record.

Please note that I am not requesting the record itself, only confirmation of its existence or non-existence. Thus, Civil Code § 1798.90.55, law enforcement exemptions, the public interest exemption or any other laws or exemptions do not apply to, and are not relevant to, this request.

A:  Per Article 1, Section 3(b)(2) of the California Constitution, broadly construe all provisions that further the public’s right to access, and apply any limitations on access as narrowly as possible.

B:  Per §7922.535(a), catalogue the responsive records or information, and describe their location. The identification of all records is applicable even if the record is withheld, pursuant to a lawful exemption to the CPRA, or redacted in whole or in part.

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RE: Wisconsin Open Records Request — Ames/Hoiland Altercation, March 18, 2026 — Candidates Debate

Dear Chief 

Pursuant to Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(1)(a) and the Wisconsin Public Records Law, I hereby request access to and copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the Lake Geneva Police Department relating to an altercation that occurred on March 18, 2026, between Cheri Ames and Joel Hoiland at or in connection with the Lake Geneva aldermanic candidates debate held that evening.

RECORDS REQUESTED

I request all of the following records without limitation as to form or format:

1. Incident and Police Reports
All initial incident reports, supplemental reports, narratives, and the final investigative report and conclusions prepared by any officer involved in responding to or investigating the March 18, 2026 altercation.

2. Video and Audio Recordings
All video recordings, body-worn camera footage, dashcam recordings, surveillance video, and any other audio or visual recordings captured by Lake Geneva Police Department personnel in connection with this incident, including recordings made at or near the debate venue.

3. Witness and Party Statements
All written, recorded, or transcribed statements obtained from any party, witness, or individual in connection with the investigation of this altercation, including but not limited to statements from Cheri Ames, Joel Hoiland, and civilian witness Joe Esposito.

4. Joe Esposito Witness Statement and Related Records
All records relating to the statement provided by civilian witness Joe Esposito, including his written or recorded statement, any follow-up communications between Mr. Esposito and the Department, and any records reflecting how his account was considered in the investigation.

5. Communications
All internal communications, emails, text messages, radio logs, CAD (Computer Aided Dispatch) records, and dispatch audio relating to the incident, including the initial call for service, officer response, and any subsequent departmental communications regarding the matter.

6. Investigative Notes and Materials
All field notes, investigative memoranda, photographs, and any other documents created or collected in the course of investigating this incident.

7. Final Report and Conclusions
The final police department report, findings, and conclusions resulting from the investigation of this altercation, including any supervisory review or approval of those conclusions.

8. Disposition Records
Any records reflecting the outcome of the investigation, including any referrals to the Walworth County District Attorney's office, charging decisions, declination of prosecution, or other final disposition of the matter.

FORMAT AND DELIVERY

I request that records be provided in electronic format (PDF or native digital format) via email. If any records exist only in physical form, I request legible copies. If electronic delivery is not practicable, please advise and I will arrange for alternative delivery.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Gary Milliette

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Lake Geneva Wisconsin Canidates Forum Altercation
communications.communication:
Gary - In response to your open records request dated April 1, 2026, for “Ames/Hoiland altercation, March 18, 2026 – candidates debate,”  I will be forwarding you a link from Axon/Evidence.com, which contains the call detail reports, bodycam videos and audio recordings pertaining to cases 26-4399, 26-4661, 26-4695, 26-4697 and 26-4702. Because the election is next week, I felt forwarding you all the recent complaints concerning city council members promptly would be helpful.

If you would like a more extensive search completed for the items not included in this link, please let me know.  I am also attaching a memo that the Chief put out to our department concerning this matter.

In an effort to protect all persons involved in this case, personally identifiable information as described in Wisconsin State Statute 19.62(5), i.e.: telephone number, street address, VIN and date of birth have been redacted pursuant to § 19.35(1)(am) in all police reports for all subjects. Additionally, the squad computers were blurred as there was information returned from DOT on the screen.  The DPPA (Driver Privacy Protection Act) prohibits a DMV from disclosing records that contain personal information that identifies an individual. This includes: name, address, telephone number, driver identification number, social security number, photographs, and medical or disability information. In an effort to comply with the legal requirements set forth by the DPPA, the following information is redacted by our department prior to any record being released: street and/or mailing address, city, telephone number(s), date of birth, driver’s license number, vehicle identification number, vehicle license plate number, and any medical or disability information. As it pertains to the above listed record, all personal information was obtained from DMV records and therefore, must be redacted to comply with DPPA requirements.

I have also redacted the identity of the anonymous witness in Case 26-4661, and any information that may identify the witness for the following reason: the records custodian must consider the legislative intent to ensure that “all witnesses of crime are treated with dignity, respect, courtesy and sensitivity; and that the rights extended in [Chapter 950] to victims and witnesses of crime are honored and protected by law enforcement agencies, prosecutors and judges.” §950.01, Wis. Stats. Preventing witnesses and victims from being revictimized by disclosure outweighs the public’s right to know their identities.

Additionally, please be advised that the definition of record under the Public Records Law does not include a draft, note, or preliminary form of a document produced by a municipal employee or officer for that individual’s personal use. Wis. Stat. § 19.32(2). Thus, notes kept in a police officer’s memo book are not subject to disclosure pursuant to the Public Records Law if they are considered to be for his or her personal use or to aid his or her recollection. The officers’ notebooks were redacted from body-worn camera video for this reason. I have included the Call Details Report Chronological Order, which details summaries from officers and dispatchers of activities during a police contact. Sometimes these are called “notes”, however, because they are created as a narrative and not solely for the officers’ personal recollection, therefore, those were released.

Additionally, to protect the personal privacy and safety of department members, body-worn camera video was redacted when featuring cell phone, the inside of the police department and the exterior of anonymous witness’ residence.

As I am sure you are aware, if you are not satisfied with this response, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(a), Wisconsin Statutes, bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record.  Additionally, you may, pursuant to 19.37(1)(b)m, request the Walworth County District Attorney or the Attorney General to bring an action for Mandamus asking a court to order the release of the record.
